stories.title: Certifications, Accreditations and Memberships
stories.summary: Clearway Group has published a comprehensive overview of its industry certifications, accreditations, and memberships, highlighting its commitment to compliance, safety, and quality standards across its security and environmental service divisions.
stories.why_it_matters: Maintaining a rigorous portfolio of accreditations—including ISO standards, BAFE, and NSI Gold—is critical for a security and property services firm to demonstrate operational excellence, regulatory compliance, and risk management to institutional and commercial clients.
verified_facts: Clearway Group holds multiple ISO certifications including 9001:2015, 14001:2015, 45001:2018, and 27001.
The group maintains industry-specific accreditations including BAFE SP203 and SP207, NSI Gold, and SSAIB approval.
Clearway is a member of the British Security Industry Association (BSIA) and the British Institute of Cleaning Science (BICSc).
warnings: The source text lists a wide range of certifications across different business units; ensure specific claims are attributed to the correct subsidiary if referenced in future communications.

drafts.concise: Clearway Group continues to demonstrate its commitment to operational excellence through a robust framework of industry certifications and accreditations. From ISO 9001, 14001, and 45001 standards to specialist BAFE and NSI Gold approvals, these credentials underscore the firm's dedication to quality, safety, and data security. By maintaining these rigorous standards, Clearway ensures it remains a trusted partner for complex security and environmental service requirements across the UK. We are pleased to see the team’s ongoing focus on compliance and professional standards. #ClearwayGroup #SecurityServices #Compliance
drafts.investor: Operational rigour is a cornerstone of sustainable growth. Clearway Group’s extensive portfolio of accreditations—including ISO 27001 for information security and NSI Gold for electronic security systems—provides a clear competitive advantage in the UK property and site security market. These third-party validations not only ensure regulatory compliance but also serve as a benchmark for service quality, reinforcing the company’s position as a reliable partner for commercial and public sector clients. This commitment to high standards is essential for long-term value creation and market trust. #Investment #OperationalExcellence #ClearwayGroup
drafts.people: At the heart of Clearway Group’s service delivery is a deep-seated commitment to safety and professional standards. The team’s dedication to achieving and maintaining a wide range of industry accreditations—such as CHAS, Constructionline, and RoSPA membership—reflects their focus on protecting people, property, and assets. These certifications are a testament to the hard work of the entire Clearway team in upholding the highest levels of quality and compliance every day. Congratulations to everyone involved in maintaining these vital industry benchmarks. #TeamWork #SafetyFirst #ClearwayGroup

stories.title: About Clearway
stories.summary: Clearway is a UK-based security and protection group providing integrated services including physical security, technology-led monitoring, safety systems, and financial recovery for high-risk environments.
stories.why_it_matters: It provides a clear overview of the operational scope and market position of a portfolio company, highlighting its integrated approach to asset protection across sectors like construction, infrastructure, and property management.
verified_facts: Clearway provides integrated security services including CCTV, alarm systems, manned guarding, and mobile patrols.
The group supports sectors including construction, property management, infrastructure, utilities, and the public sector.
The Clearway Group was formally established as a holding company in 2016, with roots dating back to 1992.
warnings: The source text is a general 'About Us' page rather than a specific news event, so the content is descriptive rather than time-sensitive.

drafts.concise: Clearway continues to provide comprehensive security and safety solutions for organisations managing complex, high-value assets across the UK. From vacant property protection and construction site security to advanced technology-led monitoring and financial recovery services, the group offers an integrated approach to risk management. By combining physical security expertise with real-time monitoring capabilities, Clearway supports clients in the construction, infrastructure, and public sectors to maintain safe and compliant environments. We are proud to support the team as they continue to deliver these essential services to their diverse client base. #Clearway #SecurityServices #UKBusiness
drafts.investor: Clearway’s operational model demonstrates the value of integrating physical security with technology-led monitoring to address the needs of high-risk environments. By serving critical sectors such as infrastructure, utilities, and property management, the group has established a robust position in the UK security market. The business continues to focus on delivering scalable, compliance-focused solutions that help organisations protect assets throughout their lifecycle. This integrated service offering, supported by a strong portfolio of accreditations, remains central to their ongoing strategy of providing reliable, data-driven security and safety outcomes for their clients. #Investment #AssetProtection #Growth
drafts.people: At the heart of Clearway’s success is a commitment to maintaining high standards of service and safety across the UK. Whether securing construction sites, protecting vacant buildings, or providing critical monitoring services, the team’s expertise is essential to their clients' operational continuity. We recognise the dedication of the professionals across The Clearway Group who work to ensure that complex environments remain secure and compliant. Their focus on professional accreditations and client-centric service continues to drive the group’s reputation for reliability in the security sector. #TeamSuccess #SecurityIndustry #ProfessionalExcellence

stories.title: Big News for Islington: Your Post Office Is Getting an Upgrade (and a New Best Friend)
stories.summary: UOE is relocating its Islington Post Office to a new, larger site at 129E Upper Street within the Islington Square shopping centre, effective 14th July. The new location integrates a modernised Post Office with the fourth 'Stamp' retail store, offering a curated range of stationery, gifts, and expanded courier services. The site features step-free access and extended seven-day opening hours.
stories.why_it_matters: The expansion demonstrates UOE's commitment to revitalising high street services by combining essential community infrastructure with a modern, customer-focused retail experience. The investment highlights the company's operational growth and its strategy to increase accessibility and service utility for local residents.
verified_facts: The new Islington Post Office and Stamp store opens on 14th July at 129E Upper Street, London N1 1QP.
The site offers extended seven-day opening hours, including Sundays.
Services include Post Office facilities, printing, scanning, foreign currency, and courier drop-offs.
This is the fourth Stamp store opened by UOE.
warnings: The article mentions a 2026 copyright date, which may indicate a discrepancy in the source material's metadata.

drafts.concise: We are pleased to share that our portfolio company, UOE, is expanding its presence in Islington. From 14th July, the local Post Office will move to a new, larger location at 129E Upper Street. This site will feature a fully modernised Post Office alongside the fourth 'Stamp' retail store, offering a curated selection of stationery and gifts. With extended seven-day opening hours and a comprehensive range of courier and administrative services, this development represents a significant upgrade for the local community. Congratulations to the UOE team on this successful expansion. #UOE #RetailInnovation #Islington
drafts.investor: UOE continues to demonstrate its commitment to high street revitalisation with the opening of its latest integrated retail and service hub in Islington. By co-locating a modernised Post Office with the 'Stamp' retail brand, UOE is effectively increasing footfall and service utility. This strategic move, which includes extended seven-day trading hours and a broader suite of courier and business services, reflects the company's focus on operational efficiency and community-led growth. We look forward to seeing the impact of this new location as UOE continues to scale its unique retail model. #Investment #Growth #RetailStrategy
drafts.people: A fantastic milestone for the UOE team as they prepare to open their new Islington Square location on 14th July. This project is a great example of how the team is reimagining the high street experience, blending essential community services with a modern retail environment. By listening to local needs and extending opening hours to seven days a week, UOE is ensuring that vital services remain accessible and convenient. Well done to Elliot Jacobs and the entire team for their hard work in bringing this new space to life. #TeamSuccess #CommunityImpact #UOE

stories.title: Molinare hires Des Carey for Dublin studio
stories.summary: Molinare Creative Group has appointed Des Carey as Executive Director, Growth & Innovation for its Dublin-based studio, Elephant Goldfish.
stories.why_it_matters: The appointment signals a strategic focus on international expansion and the integration of emerging production technologies within the Molinare Creative Group, leveraging Carey's extensive background in global cinema innovation.
verified_facts: Des Carey has been appointed as Executive Director, Growth & Innovation at Elephant Goldfish.
Elephant Goldfish is a member of the Molinare Creative Group.
Des Carey previously held senior roles at Company 3, Light Iron, and Samsung.
warnings: The article mentions a date of 16.07.26, which appears to be a future-dated or placeholder timestamp in the source material.

drafts.concise: We are pleased to share that the Molinare Creative Group has appointed Des Carey as Executive Director, Growth & Innovation for its Dublin-based studio, Elephant Goldfish. With over 25 years of experience across production, post-production, and technology, Des will lead strategic growth and partnership initiatives. This appointment marks a significant step in the Group’s ongoing expansion and commitment to integrating emerging production technologies. We look forward to seeing the impact of his leadership on the team in Dublin and across the wider Group. #Molinare #ElephantGoldfish #MediaProduction
drafts.investor: The Molinare Creative Group continues to strengthen its leadership team with the appointment of Des Carey as Executive Director, Growth & Innovation at Elephant Goldfish. This strategic hire underscores the Group’s commitment to professionalisation and international growth. By bringing in expertise in cloud-based infrastructure and next-generation cinema technology, the business is well-positioned to capitalise on evolving industry demands. This move reflects a clear ambition to scale operations and enhance the Group’s competitive position in the global post-production market. #Investment #GrowthStrategy #PostProduction
drafts.people: A warm welcome to Des Carey, who joins the Molinare Creative Group as Executive Director, Growth & Innovation at Elephant Goldfish. Des brings a wealth of international experience, having worked with industry leaders at Lucasfilm, Industrial Light & Magic, and Samsung. His expertise in cinematic innovation and virtual production will be a tremendous asset to the creative and commercial teams in Dublin. We are proud to support the Molinare Creative Group as they continue to attract top-tier talent to drive their vision forward. #Leadership #Innovation #Molinare

stories.title: Former ILM exec to drive growth in Molinare’s Irish operations - TVBEurope
stories.summary: Molinare Creative Group has appointed a former Industrial Light & Magic (ILM) executive to lead and expand its operations in Ireland.
stories.why_it_matters: This strategic hire signals Molinare's commitment to scaling its international footprint and leveraging high-level industry expertise to drive growth in the Irish post-production market.
verified_facts: Molinare is expanding its operations in Ireland.
A former ILM executive has been appointed to lead this growth.
warnings: The specific name of the executive was not provided in the source material.

drafts.concise: We are pleased to share that Molinare Creative Group is strengthening its international presence with the appointment of a former Industrial Light & Magic executive to lead its Irish operations. This strategic move marks an important step in the group's ongoing development and commitment to delivering world-class post-production services. Congratulations to the team at Molinare on this significant addition to their leadership. #Molinare #PostProduction #BusinessGrowth
drafts.investor: Molinare Creative Group continues to execute its growth strategy with the appointment of a former ILM executive to spearhead its Irish operations. By attracting top-tier talent with significant international experience, Molinare is well-positioned to scale its service offering and enhance its competitive standing in the post-production sector. We look forward to seeing the impact of this leadership on the group's expansion plans in the region. #Investment #Molinare #CreativeIndustries
drafts.people: Attracting high-calibre talent is essential to building a world-class creative business. Molinare Creative Group has taken a significant step forward by appointing a former ILM executive to drive its operations in Ireland. Bringing in leadership with such deep industry expertise is a testament to the group's reputation and its ambition to grow its footprint. We are proud to support the Molinare team as they continue to build their capabilities. #Leadership #Molinare #MediaProduction

stories.title: Mint Explainer | Why Facebook copyright system is under scrutiny in Delhi HC
stories.summary: McGraw Hill is named as one of several publishers involved in a class-action lawsuit against Meta and its CEO, Mark Zuckerberg, regarding the alleged unauthorised use of copyrighted books and journal articles to train Llama AI models.
stories.why_it_matters: The litigation highlights the ongoing legal and ethical challenges surrounding the use of copyrighted intellectual property in the development of generative AI models, a critical area of focus for content-heavy businesses.
verified_facts: McGraw Hill is a plaintiff in a class-action lawsuit against Meta and Mark Zuckerberg.
The lawsuit alleges that Meta illegally copied copyrighted books and journal articles to train Llama AI models without permission or payment.
warnings: The article focuses primarily on a separate legal dispute involving Indian content creators and Meta; the mention of McGraw Hill is secondary and provides limited detail on the status of the publisher's specific class-action claim.
The information regarding the class-action lawsuit is attributed to a New York Times report cited within the Mint article, rather than a direct source from McGraw Hill.

drafts.concise: McGraw Hill is currently participating in a class-action lawsuit against Meta regarding the use of copyrighted materials in AI model training. The legal action, which includes several other major publishers, centres on allegations that copyrighted books and journal articles were used to train Llama AI models without authorisation. As the role of intellectual property in the development of generative AI continues to be a significant point of discussion across the publishing and technology sectors, we continue to monitor these developments closely. Protecting the integrity of original content remains a fundamental priority for the industry. #McGrawHill #IntellectualProperty #AI
drafts.investor: The intersection of intellectual property rights and generative AI development remains a critical strategic consideration for content-focused businesses. McGraw Hill is currently involved in a class-action lawsuit against Meta, challenging the use of copyrighted books and journals to train Llama AI models without appropriate licensing or compensation. This litigation underscores the importance of robust IP protection frameworks as AI technologies evolve. We remain focused on how our portfolio companies navigate these complex regulatory and legal landscapes to protect their core assets and long-term value. #InvestmentStrategy #AI #CopyrightLaw
drafts.people: The protection of intellectual property is essential to the mission of publishers like McGraw Hill. The company is currently engaged in a class-action lawsuit against Meta, asserting that its copyrighted works were used to train AI models without permission. This legal action highlights the dedication of the McGraw Hill team to defending their content and ensuring that creators and publishers are fairly recognised for their contributions to the knowledge economy. We support our portfolio companies as they address these vital challenges to safeguard their intellectual assets in an increasingly digital world. #McGrawHill #ContentRights #Innovation
